ParsedResults.ParsedText:
Solar cell
Article Talk
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
For convection cells on the Sun's surface, see Solar granule.
A solar cell or photovoltaic cell (PV cell) is an electronic device that converts the energy of light directly
into electricity by means Of the photovoltaic effect. [II It is a form Of photoelectric cell, a device whose
electrical characteristics (such as current, voltage, or resistance) vary when exposed to light. Individual
solar cell devices are often the electrical building blocks of photovoltaic modules, known colloquially as
"solar panels". The common single-junction silicon solar cell can produce a maximum Open-circuit voltage
of approximately 0.5 to 0.6 volts.(2J
Photovoltaic cells may operate under sunlight or artificial light. In addition to producing energy, they can be
used as a photodetector (for example infrared detectors), detecting light or other electromagnetic radiation
near the visible range, or measuring light intensity.
Read
Edit
75 languages v
View history Tools v

get.description:
Fetches daily FX spot rates between two currencies using official central-bank reference rates.

**Path Parameters:**
- `base`: Base currency code (e.g., AUD, USD).
- `quote`: Quote currency code (e.g., USD, EUR).

**Query Parameters:**
- `start_date`: Optional. Start date YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to 365 days ago.
- `end_date`: Optional. End date YYYY-MM-DD. Defaults to today.

- `indicators`: Optional. Comma-separated list of technical indicators to compute from the spot-rate series and include in the response. Pass `all` to include every supported indicator. Supported values: `bollinger_bands`, `ema_12`, `ema_26`, `macd`, `rsi_14`, `sma_20`, `sma_200`, `sma_50`, `all`.

**FX source and derivation metadata:**
- FX values are served from FXMacroData's Firestore-backed reference-rate store. Canonical USD quote legs are stored first; inverse and cross pairs can be derived from those legs when a requested direct pair is not stored.
- Responses include `pair_metadata` and row-level `data[].source` with `direct_available`, `inverse_available`, `is_derived`, `derived_from_inverse`, `derived_from_cross`, `derived_from_source_legs`, `derivation_method`, `anchor_currency`, `source_pair`, `source_legs`, and `pair_consistency_check`.
- Upstream FX source priority is BIS XRU daily USD exchange rates where available, then official national-bank daily sources, explicitly labelled EUR/CAD-derived daily crosses, official USD pegs, BIS monthly averages expanded to business days, IMF IFS monthly rates, and World Bank annual rates. Lower-frequency expansions are labelled with `source_frequency` and `derivation_method`; they are not presented as true daily fixings.
- `data[].observation_datetime` is the UTC midnight timestamp for the FX reference date. It is an observation-date marker, not a publication timestamp.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: Required path parameter. Base currency code Allowed values: AUD, BRL, CAD, CHF, CNH, CNY, DKK, EUR, GBP, IDR, ILS, JPY, NGN, NOK, NZD, PEN, SEK, THB, USD.

parameters.name: quote
parameters.description: Required path parameter. Quote currency code Allowed values: AUD, BRL, CAD, CHF, CNH, CNY, DKK, EUR, GBP, IDR, ILS, JPY, NGN, NOK, NZD, PEN, SEK, THB, USD.

200.description: Daily FX spot-rate series with optional computed technical indicators.

get.description:
Returns the currently available macroeconomic indicators for a specific currency.

    Availability is determined from the Firestore-backed product store rather than
    the static indicator registry alone, so temporarily unavailable series are
    excluded until live data is available again. The catalogue is refreshed on a
    regular schedule.

    This route also replaces the deprecated `/v1/capabilities` and
    `/v1/coverage_matrix` endpoints. Coverage is included directly on each
    catalogue entry. To also include the legacy top-level coverage list, call it
    with `include_coverage=true`. To include capability metadata, call it with
    `include_capabilities=true`.

    Each entry includes:
    * **name**: The full descriptive name of the metric.
    * **unit**: The unit of measurement (e.g., %YoY, Billions of USD).
    * **frequency**: How often the data is updated (Daily, Monthly, Quarterly).
    * **source** / **source_url**: The publisher or source family used for the stored series, when available.
    * **source_series_id** / **source_series_name**: The upstream source identifier and exact source-series label, when available.
    * **seasonal_adjustment**: Seasonal basis (`SA`, `NSA`, `UNKNOWN`, or `NOT_APPLICABLE`).
    * **price_basis**: GDP/level price basis (`REAL`, `NOMINAL`, or `UNKNOWN`) when applicable.
    * **aliases** / **related_indicators**: Optional flat discovery metadata for common alternative names and adjacent indicators.
    * **standardization_note**: Optional note explaining sign, unit, scope, or cross-currency comparability choices.
    * **has_official_forecast**: Whether the relevant central bank or statistics agency publishes a preliminary, flash, or advance estimate of this indicator before the main/final data release.
    * **coverage**: Earliest/latest stored dates, row count, recency status, and coverage quality for that indicator.

parameters.name: currency

schema.description: The 3-letter currency code (e.g., USD, AUD, EUR, GBP, CHF)
schema.title: Currency

200.description: Indicator metadata keyed by indicator slug, with optional capability and coverage metadata.

get.summary: Get upcoming economic data release dates
get.description:
Returns upcoming economic data release dates for a given currency.

Rows are served from Firestore-first calendar documents. Announcement rows keep the existing `release` slug mapping to `/v1/announcements/{currency}/{indicator}`, while other domains such as COT may also include additive fields like `domain`, `endpoint_family`, and `endpoint_path`.

**Path Parameters:**
- `currency`: The 3-letter currency code (e.g., USD).

**Query Parameters:**
- `indicator`: Optional. Filter to a specific indicator (e.g., gdp, inflation, unemployment, non_farm_payrolls).

- `start_date`: Optional inclusive lower bound, YYYY-MM-DD.
- `end_date`: Optional inclusive upper bound, YYYY-MM-DD.

**Returns:** List of release rows sorted by announcement time ascending (nearest scheduled release first). Core fields remain `announcement_datetime` and `release`; additive routing metadata may also be present.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Required path parameter. Allowed values: AED, ARS, AUD, BOB, BRL, CAD, CHF, CLP, CNH, CNY, COMM, COP, CZK, DKK, DZD, EGP, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, IDR, ILS, INR, JPY, KRW, MAD, MXN, MYR, NGN, NOK, NZD, PEN, PHP, PKR, PLN, RUB, SAR, SEK, SGD, THB, TRY, TWD, USD, UYU, VND, ZAR.

parameters.name: indicator

schema.description: Filter by indicator name (e.g., gdp, inflation, unemployment)
schema.title: Indicator

parameters.description: Optional query parameter. Filter by indicator name (e.g., gdp, inflation, unemployment) Allowed values: average_hourly_earnings, balance_on_goods, balance_on_services, breakeven_inflation_rate, building_approvals, building_permits, business_confidence, capital_account_balance, cb_assets, commodity_price_energy, commodity_price_ex_energy, commodity_price_index, consumer_confidence, core_inflation, core_inflation_median, core_inflation_mom, core_inflation_trim, core_pce, credit_growth, current_account_balance, deposit_rates, durable_goods_orders, employment, exports, financial_account_balance, foreign_reserves, full_time_employment, gdp, gdp_growth_q4_yoy, gdp_growth_qoq_saar, gdp_quarterly, gold_reserves, gov_bond_10y, gov_bond_1y, gov_bond_20y, gov_bond_2y, gov_bond_30y, gov_bond_3y, gov_bond_40y, gov_bond_4y, gov_bond_5y, gov_bond_7y, government_debt, house_price_index, household_credit, housing_starts, imports, inflation, inflation_linked_bond, inflation_mom, initial_jobless_claims, international_assets, international_liabilities, job_openings, m1, m2, m3, monthly_cpi, nairu, net_foreign_asset_position, non_farm_payrolls, part_time_employment, participation_rate, pce, pce_mom, policy_rate, policy_rate_mlf, policy_rate_mro, policy_rate_target_lower, ppi, ppi_mom, primary_income_balance, retail_sales, risk_free_rate, secondary_income_balance, services_balance, sight_deposits, terms_of_trade, trade_balance, trade_weighted_index, trimmed_mean_inflation, unemployment, wage_price_index, wages.

200.description: Upcoming release dates for the selected currency and optional indicator filter.

get.description:
Returns a real-time snapshot of the four major FX trading sessions (Sydney, Tokyo, London, New York) and their overlap windows.

Each session entry shows today's open and close times in UTC, whether the session is currently open, and seconds until it opens or closes.

Overlap windows highlight the highest-liquidity trading periods — in particular the **London / New York** overlap (~13:00–17:00 UTC in winter), which sees the largest volume and tightest spreads on major USD pairs.

**Query Parameters:**
- `at`: Optional ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (e.g. `2026-03-05T12:00:00Z`). Defaults to the current server time. Useful for scheduling or back-testing decisions.

**No authentication required.**

parameters.name: at
parameters.description: Optional query parameter. Optional ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (e.g. 2026-03-05T12:00:00Z)

200.description: Session snapshot including open/close state and overlap windows.

sessions.name: Sydney
sessions.description: First major session to open after the weekend.

overlaps.name: London / New York
overlaps.description: Most active window of the trading day.

get.description:
Returns a store-backed historical differential between the base and quote currency using a shared rate measure. The endpoint aligns the two legs by date intersection and reports the spread in both percentage points and basis points.

**Measures:**
- Supported values: `auto`, `risk_free_rate`, `gov_bond_2y`, `gov_bond_10y`, `policy_rate`.
- `auto` prefers `risk_free_rate`, then `gov_bond_2y`, then `policy_rate`.

**Authentication:**
- Requires a Professional API key via `?api_key=<key>` or `X-API-Key`.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: Required path parameter. Base currency code Allowed values: AUD, BRL, CAD, CHF, CNH, CNY, DKK, EUR, GBP, IDR, ILS, JPY, NGN, NOK, NZD, PEN, SEK, THB, USD.

parameters.name: quote
parameters.description: Required path parameter. Quote currency code Allowed values: AUD, BRL, CAD, CHF, CNH, CNY, DKK, EUR, GBP, IDR, ILS, JPY, NGN, NOK, NZD, PEN, SEK, THB, USD.

200.description: Successful Response

get.description:
Returns the latest stored official-source curve nodes for the requested currency and curve family. The current implementation serves government nominal curve nodes already ingested into the FXMacroData store.

**Curve Families:**
- Supported values: `government_nominal`.

**Metrics:**
- Supported values: `spot`.

**Authentication:**
- Requires a Professional API key via `?api_key=<key>` or `X-API-Key`.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Required path parameter. Currency code Allowed values: ARS, AUD, BRL, CAD, CHF, CNH, CNY, CZK, DKK, EUR, GBP, HKD, HUF, IDR, ILS, INR, ISK, JPY, MXN, MYR, NGN, NOK, NZD, PEN, PLN, SEK, SGD, THB, TRY, USD, XOF, ZAR.

parameters.name: curve_family
parameters.description: Optional query parameter. Official curve family to return. Allowed values: government_nominal.

200.description: Successful Response

announcement_id.title: Announcement Id
announcement_id.description: Stable identifier of the announcement these predictions target, in the form ``{currency}_{indicator}_{date}``.

date.title: Date
date.description: Reference period date of the forecast, ``YYYY-MM-DD``.

announcement_datetime.title: Announcement Datetime
announcement_datetime.description: Release timestamp of the forecasted announcement, Unix epoch seconds (UTC).

announcement_datetime_local.title: Announcement Datetime Local
announcement_datetime_local.description: ISO 8601 release time with timezone offset, rendered in the currency's market timezone.

announcement_timing.title: Announcement Timing
announcement_timing.description: Whether the forecasted announcement is still upcoming (`future`) or already released (`past`).

predictions.title: Predictions
predictions.description: List of forecasts for this announcement, one entry per prediction source.  Sorted by ``prediction_source`` for deterministic output.

AnnouncementPredictions.title: AnnouncementPredictions
AnnouncementPredictions.description:
All available forecasts targeting a single announcement.

``announcement_id`` matches the value surfaced on the corresponding
row in ``/v1/announcements/{currency}/{indicator}``, allowing clients
to join predictions with realised observations.  ``predictions``
contains one sub-entry per source (e.g. central-bank projection,
IMF WEO, market consensus survey, FXMacroData prediction).

resource.title: Resource
resource.description: Optional protected resource identifier sent by some MCP OAuth clients.

CBTargetEntry.title: CBTargetEntry
CBTargetEntry.description: A single central-bank target entry valid from ``effective_from``.

CBTargetInfo.title: CBTargetInfo
CBTargetInfo.description:
Central-bank target metadata for an indicator.

``current`` is the entry effective as of the response ``end_date``.
``history`` contains every known target regime in ascending date order.

tags.description: Primary product: macroeconomic announcement time series.

info.title: YMove Fitness API
info.description: Access 698+ professional exercise videos, a comprehensive food & nutrition database, recipe search, and AI-powered meal plan generation. Features include rate limiting, monthly exercise caps, and video access protection. Video URLs are pre-signed and expire after 48 hours. Always fetch fresh exercise data before displaying videos. Sign up at https://ymove.app/exercise-api/signup for a free trial.

Video.description: Video URLs are pre-signed and expire after 48 hours. Do not cache these URLs. Always fetch fresh exercise data before displaying videos.

videoUrl.description: Pre-signed CDN URL for MP4 video. Expires after 48 hours.

videoHlsUrl.description: Pre-signed CDN URL for HLS playlist. Expires after 48 hours.

thumbnailUrl.description: Pre-signed CDN URL for thumbnail image. Expires after 48 hours.

orientation.description: Video aspect orientation. Most exercise clips are PORTRAIT (vertical, ~9:16), shot for mobile. Do not assume landscape. Read this field and size your player to a portrait container (e.g. aspect-ratio 9/16) with object-fit: cover so clips fill the frame instead of letterboxing.

example.title: Barbell Bench Press
example.description: A compound chest exercise performed lying on a flat bench.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder width
importantPoints: Keep your back flat on the bench
Do not bounce the bar off your chest

muscleGroups.description: Muscle groups targeted (expanded from any aliases in the request).

muscleGroupsRequested.description: The original muscleGroup values from the request, including any aliases (full_body, upper_body, lower_body).

estimatedMinutes.description: Estimated total workout duration, including warmup and cooldown when present.

warmup.description: Warmup exercises (bodyweight stretches, mobility, and light cardio) placed at the start of the session. Present unless includeWarmup=false.
Warmup exercises (bodyweight stretches, mobility, and light cardio) placed at the start of this day. Present unless includeWarmup=false.

cooldown.description: Cooldown exercises (bodyweight stretches and mobility) placed at the end of this day. Present unless includeCooldown=false.

When the monthly exercise cap is exceeded, exercises not previously accessed will have video properties (videoUrl, videoHlsUrl, thumbnailUrl, videoDurationSecs, videos) removed entirely. A `_warning` object is included with details. Previously accessed exercises keep their video URLs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Exercise UUID or slug

200.description: Exercise details. When the monthly exercise cap is exceeded and this exercise was not previously accessed, video properties are removed entirely and a `_warning` is included.

parameters.name: muscleGroup
parameters.description: Target muscle group(s). Accepts a single value, a comma-separated list (e.g. "chest,back"), or repeated query keys. Aliases: "full_body" (all groups), "upper_body" (chest, back, shoulders, biceps, triceps, forearms, core), "lower_body" (quads, hamstrings, glutes, calves). Aliases can be combined with specific groups.

parameters.name: equipment
parameters.description: Limit to specific equipment. Accepts a single value, comma-separated list, or repeated keys.

200.description: Generated workout. When the monthly exercise cap is exceeded, exercises not previously accessed will have video properties removed entirely. A `_warning` object is included with details.

parameters.name: goal
parameters.description: Training goal. Sets the set/rep/rest scheme and exercises-per-muscle.

parameters.name: difficulty
parameters.description: Difficulty level. Filters which exercises are selected per muscle: beginner returns beginner-level moves, intermediate returns beginner+intermediate, advanced prefers advanced moves (falling back to intermediate where advanced options are limited). Combine with goal, which controls volume.

200.description: Generated training program. When the monthly exercise cap is exceeded, exercises not previously accessed will have video properties removed entirely. A `_warning` object is included with details.

post.description: Upload an exercise video and get AI-powered form analysis. Returns a form score (0-100), detected issues with plain-language fixes, and corrective exercise recommendations.

type.description: How the video is provided: base64-encoded data or a public URL.

data.description: Base64-encoded video data, or a public URL to the video file.

exercise_name.description: Name of the exercise being performed (e.g. "barbell squat", "deadlift").

custom_instructions.description: Optional extra instructions for the analysis (e.g. "focus on knee alignment").

num_frames.description: Number of frames to extract from the video for analysis.

200.description: Form analysis result with score, issues, and corrective exercises
